key_insight: A symmetry-adapted single-orbital tight-binding model can reproduce the low-energy band structure and spin splitting of chiral 1D lead halide perovskites using only a small set of effective hopping terms.
engineering_relevance: This provides a compact, interpretable Hamiltonian for predicting and tuning band-edge electronic, spin, optical, and transport properties in chiral perovskite devices.
method: Built a single-orbital tight-binding Hamiltonian on four symmetry-related sites per primitive unit cell.
Included layer, in-plane sublattice, and spin degrees of freedom with symmetry-adapted spin-dependent hoppings.
Used separate parameter sets for conduction and valence bands and analyzed degeneracies via screw eigenvalues and antiunitary symmetries.
evidence_signal: The model is benchmarked against density-functional-theory band dispersions and spin polarizations, and it quantitatively captures the band-edge spin splittings.
watch_item: Because the model uses limited symmetry-adapted terms and separate conduction/valence parameter sets, it may miss physics outside the low-energy regime or effects requiring additional orbitals/interactions.
paper_title: Single-orbital tight-binding model for chiral one-dimensional hybrid organic-inorganic lead halide perovskites
